intake_record.name: Ahmed Al-Hassan
intake_record.special_needs: Two young children (5, 7) also showing respiratory symptoms

action_plan: MODERATE TRIAGE: Community health worker visit within 24 hours. Suspected respiratory infection (possibly outbreak in settlement). Priority 1: Isolate family to observation tent. Provide antimalarial course + ORS packets for dehydration. Monitor children for worsening symptoms. Priority 2: Distribute food rations (high-calorie) and ensure water access. Link to camp health clinic for follow-up in 48 hours.

resources.name: Refugee Camp Health Clinic
resources.capacity: Community health worker available, telemedicine link to physician

resources.name: Camp Food Distribution
resources.capacity: High-energy rations available, cultural preferences documented

evidence.content: Respiratory infections in crowded displacement settings require rapid isolation and contact tracing. Monitor for secondary cases in close quarters. ORS and nutritional support are critical for recovery.
All affected persons have equitable access to a health service. Services shall be provided regardless of ability to pay. Preventive care is prioritized for vulnerable populations.
